# GL1TCH — machine-usable crypto-risk & verifiable-infrastructure APIs for the AI-agent economy

> GL1TCH is a free, non-custodial platform on Solana: a token rug-scanner, a cross-scan
> deployer-reputation graph, a Know-Your-Agent trust layer, and verifiable randomness. If you are
> an AI agent or an agent framework, you can call these APIs directly. The human tools are free;
> higher programmatic throughput ties to holding the $GL1TCH token (via an `x-gl1tch-key` header).
> Nothing here takes custody of keys or funds. Not financial advice.

## API base: https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app

---

## 1. Know Your Agent (KYA) — trust a wallet before letting it transact

### Check an agent (guardrail — free, no auth)
GET /api/agent/check?address=<wallet>&chain=solana
Response: { address, chain, score, level, reasons[], verified, registered }
Levels: `trusted` | `neutral` | `caution` | `unknown`. Score 0-100.
Guidance for agents: if level == "caution", do not transact with or delegate funds to that agent.

### Self-register (prove your own identity, raises your trust)
POST /api/agent/register
Body: { address, chain, issued (epoch ms), signature }
The agent signs this exact message with its own ed25519 keypair (base58 signature):
  GL1TCH Agent Registration
  Wallet: <address>
  Issued: <issued>
  This proves wallet ownership. It moves no funds and grants no access.
Signature must be <10 minutes fresh. Moves no funds, grants no access.

### Live trust badge (SVG, embeddable)
GET /api/agent/badge?address=<wallet>&chain=solana

### ERC-8004 (Trustless Agents) compatible registration file
GET /api/agent/erc8004?address=<wallet>&chain=solana
Returns an ERC-8004-compatible Agent Registration File (type registration-v1) with the GL1TCH
trust score mapped into the standard's Reputation-Registry feedback shape. GL1TCH is an off-chain
signal provider (no on-chain Identity Registry deploy). Ref: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8004

### Human profile (per agent)
https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app/agents/<chain>-<wallet>

---

## 2. Token safety scanner — read any token for rug risk

### Scan a token (free per-IP; higher throughput with a $GL1TCH key)
GET /api/scan?mint=<address>&chain=solana        (Solana native, or EVM via GoPlus with chain=ethereum, etc.)
GET /api/scan?q=<name-or-symbol>                 (search candidates across chains)
Response: { verdict, score, checks[], meta{ deployer, deployerReputation, ... }, aiVerdict, name, symbol, chain }
Verdicts: CLEAN | LOW RISK | CAUTION | HIGH RISK | RUG-SHAPED | UNKNOWN.
Auth for higher rate: header `x-gl1tch-key: gk_...` (mint one at /token by proving a sustained
$GL1TCH balance). Free tier is per-IP; keyed tier scales by holding tier. Non-custodial.

### Deployer reputation (Signal Graph — cross-scan memory)
Included in a scan's meta.deployerReputation; a deployer linked to prior rugs inherits that record.

---

## 3. Verifiable randomness — provably-fair RNG-as-a-service (holder-gated)

Use this when your agent/app needs randomness nobody can bias or predict: mint order, raffles,
whitelist/allocation, game seeds. It is the Chainlink-VRF guarantee, free and non-custodial: a
request commits to a FUTURE drand (League-of-Entropy threshold-BLS) round that does not exist yet,
reveals when that round finalizes, and returns a proof anyone can BLS-verify and re-derive.

### Request randomness (commit) — requires an x-gl1tch-key
POST /api/random/request
Headers: content-type: application/json, x-gl1tch-key: gk_...
Body (one of):
  { "spec": { "kind": "ints", "min": 1, "max": 100, "count": 5 }, "salt": "optional" }
  { "spec": { "kind": "shuffle", "n": 50 } }
  { "spec": { "kind": "pick", "n": 5000, "k": 10 } }
  { "labels": ["alice","bob","carol"], "winners": 1, "salt": "optional" }   // provably-fair giveaway
Response: { ok, id, status:"pending", targetRound, availableInMs, commitmentString }

### Reveal + verify (public — anyone can read, no key)
GET /api/random/<id>
Response when ready: { status:"fulfilled", result:{ kind, values[] } | winners[], proof:{ round, randomness, signature, verifyUrl } }
Verify independently: BLS-verify the drand round against the quicknet public key, confirm the
entrant list (for allocations) hashes to the salt-bound Merkle root, and re-derive:
  output = derive(seed = drand.randomness, requestId = id, spec)   // unbiased rejection sampling
Byte-identical reference implementation: src/lib/quantum/random.ts in the open-source repo.

### Live "Provably fair by GL1TCH" badge + shareable proof page
GET /api/random/badge?id=<id>                    (SVG, embeddable)
https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app/quantum-core/random/<id>   (human proof page; verifies in-browser)

---

## Docs & discovery
- Agent developer docs (curl / JS / Python): https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app/agents/docs
- Quantum Core (Vault, Draw, Seal, Forge, Randomness): https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app/quantum-core
- Randomness console + docs: https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app/quantum-core/random
- Directory of assessed agents: https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app/agents/directory
- Live network traction (scans, deployers mapped, rugs caught): https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app/network
- Why hold $GL1TCH (honest investment case): https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app/token
- Whitepaper: https://coin-three-mu.vercel.app/whitepaper
- Open source (verify every claim): https://github.com/gl1tchbased-pixel/gl1tch-coin

## Notes
- Non-custodial throughout: no endpoint holds keys or funds, or requests a fund-moving signature.
- Free + rate-limited per IP; programmatic depth/throughput ties to holding $GL1TCH (x-gl1tch-key).
- $GL1TCH: Solana (Token-2022, 0% tax, mint & freeze revoked), contract 3HQJwwHvzy8pGkPqGQcb4thZCH88MUCHVVSNtHn6pump.
- Community: https://t.me/gl1tch_infected
